commands.review_summary:
The PR adds safe boundary reads for plugin-owned provider catalog, model, auth, and paired-provider rows so malformed rows are omitted while healthy sibling output remains visible.

PR surface: Source +124, Tests +159. Total +283 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main uses Object.entries and direct auth/model field reads, and the PR adds focused throwing-getter/proxy tests that exercise the failure path. I did not run the tests in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The protected maintainer label, draft state, and compatibility/auth-provider semantics make this a human landing decision rather than a safe automated repair job.

target.title: fix(plugin-sdk): isolate provider catalog projection failures
target.body:
## Summary

- Isolates malformed plugin-owned provider catalog rows so one bad provider/model/auth entry does not erase healthy sibling output.
- Adds a small shared safe-record helper for boundary reads that must tolerate unreadable plugin-provided rows.
- Intentionally excludes broader exotic fuzz cases from the earlier draft; this PR keeps only the catalog-row isolation contract.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: unreadable provider catalog, model, auth, or paired-provider rows are skipped without dropping valid sibling provider catalog output.
- Real environment tested: focused local Vitest wrapper on current main; AWS Crabbox/Testbox changed gate on the current pushed head.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugin-sdk/provider-entry.test.ts src/plugins/provider-catalog.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
  - `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
  - `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check <changed files>`
  - private-name/private-path branch scrub
  - `OPENCLAW_TESTBOX=1 node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"`
- Evidence after fix: focused Vitest passed 1 shard / 14 tests at head `7e96af52bffb8e409cbcc2bbeaefaeac8550c640`; oxfmt passed; diff check passed; scrub returned no matches; AWS Crabbox `run_0c99f3172958` on lease `cbx_66cfdf117302` passed `pnpm check:changed` with lanes `core`, `coreTests`, `extensions`, and `extensionTests`.
- Observed result after fix: malformed plugin-owned rows are omitted while healthy provider catalog entries remain visible.
- What was not tested: live provider authentication against an external service; this is a catalog projection hardening path with synthetic `fuzzplugin`/`mockplugin` fixture rows.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: none for the touched contract path.

## Tests and validation

Commands run:

- `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugin-sdk/provider-entry.test.ts src/plugins/provider-catalog.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check <changed files>`
- private-name/private-path branch scrub
- `OPENCLAW_TESTBOX=1 node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"`

Regression coverage added:

- unreadable provider catalog entries preserve healthy siblings
- unreadable model rows preserve healthy siblings
- unreadable auth option rows preserve healthy entries
- unreadable paired provider entries are omitted without failing the catalog

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Malformed provider plugin metadata is now skipped instead of poisoning the whole projected provider catalog.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Provider catalog/auth projection now fails around bad plugin-owned rows instead of failing the full catalog path.

What is the highest-risk area?

Plugin SDK provider catalog compatibility for malformed third-party plugin metadata.

How is that risk mitigated?

The behavior is scoped to unreadable plugin-owned rows. Valid rows keep the same shape, and the shared helper only treats non-record/unreadable rows as omitted.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Draft PR for maintainer review and CI. It can land independently after #88368 or before it if reviewers prefer the provider-catalog hardening first.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

GitHub CI and maintainer review.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

Local structured review was previously clean after pruning; the refreshed branch also passed the remote changed gate on the current head.
